title: In the Night Dimension and Those We Meet at Twilight
description: Strange beings lurk in pixelated places, eyes peep from dark spaces, and seemingly random objects confront curious travelers. These are the dreamscapes that visitors will encounter in artist Jon Hioki’s new exhibition, In the Night Dimension and Those You Meet at Twilight, opening June 20th at Wonder Docent. The show, which features original paintings and sculptures, will be on display at the downtown Nevada City art supply store through August 16th., Wonder Docent will host an opening reception for In the Night Dimension and Those You Meet at Twilight on Saturday, June 20th from 6 to 8 pm.

title: Summer Nature Pop Art Classes
description: Nature Pop Art classes, all ages, $75 for 3 sessions (2 hours each).

title: Kiki’s Delivery Service 4K
venue_name: The Onyx Downtown at the Nevada Theatre
description: 7/5 - The Onyx Downtown’s annual celebration of the beloved Japanese animation studio returns to the Nevada Theatre this summer, with a brand new slate for Studio Ghibli in July! First released in 1989, KIKI’S DELIVERY SERVICE is one of Director Hayao Miyazaki’s most adored features from Studio Ghibli. The film, presented in a brand new 4K restoration, follows a 13-year-old witch named Kiki, who leaves home for a coastal town to undergo her traditional year of independent training. Accompanied by her talking black cat Jiji, she utilizes her ability to fly and launches a courier business, ultimately navigating the challenges of self-discovery and burnout.

title: Silly Sculptures & Fun Figures Youth Maker XD Camp Experience
description: Join us for a colorful, hands-on adventure in sculpting, shaping, and imagining!, Polymer clay is the perfect, no-mess medium for kids of all ages to explore their creativity. In this fun and flexible workshop, young makers will experiment with different texturing tools, colors, and techniques to bring their ideas to life – from silly creatures and tiny treasures to action figurines and imaginative keepsakes.. Children are encouraged to use their imaginations and create whatever they like.

title: Hand Carving Wooden Spoons Youth Maker XD Camp Experience
description: Description, Come experience the timeless craft of carving and discover the joy of making something both simple and enduring., There’s something deeply satisfying about shaping wood with your own two hands., In this beginner-friendly workshop, you’ll start with a simple block of wood and transform it into a beautiful, functional wooden utensil using traditional hand tools. Under expert guidance, you’ll learn how to safely and confidently use an axe, carving knife, and scoop knife to shape, hollow, and refine your piece.

title: Glass Fusing & Flameworking Youth Maker XD Camp Experience
description: This one-of-a-kind camp introduces young artists to two captivating glass art techniques — glass fusing and glass flameworking with a torch — giving campers a rare opportunity to work with real glass-studio tools and bring home stunning, handmade pieces they’ll be proud to display.

title: In the Night Dimension and Those We Meet at Twilight
description: Strange beings lurk in pixelated places, eyes peep from dark spaces, and seemingly random objects confront curious travelers. These are the dreamscapes that visitors will encounter in artist Jon Hioki’s new exhibition, In the Night Dimension and Those You Meet at Twilight, opening June 20th at Wonder Docent. The show, which features original paintings and sculptures, will be on display at the downtown Nevada City art supply store through August 16th., Wonder Docent will host an opening reception for In the Night Dimension and Those You Meet at Twilight on Saturday, June 20th from 6 to 8 pm.

title: Lavender Glow | Queer Art & Our Shared History
description: Beginning with Nisenan history into the present day, Lavender Glow reflects upon and celebrates the lived experiences and legacies of queer individuals and groups whose collective influence demonstrate a profound investment in place, arts, and culture. Alongside highlights of local history, Lavender Glow features 44 artworks selected from 127 submissions.

description: The most relaxed, most welcoming open mic in Western Nevada County

title: African Drum and Dance of Nevada County
description: Come join our vibrant African Drum and Dance classes! Open to all ages and abilities, we explore different styles from across the African Diasporic disciplines. Including Malian, Congolese, Guinean, Senegalese, Afro-Cuban and Afro-Brazilian. Masters of their craft travel from all over to share their culture and heritage with our beautiful community, along with our own fabulous local teachers and percussionists, to offer a truly inspiring and engaging experience

title: "Musical Mornings" on Whimsical Wednesdays - Vocal Locals
description: Free to the public, Musical Mornings is a popular, family-friendly summer series of one-hour morning concerts for listeners of all ages. The atmosphere is casual, with attendees bringing their own lawn chairs or blankets and snacks. Kids are encouraged to wiggle, dance, sing, and play., The summer series, presented by InConcert Sierra, the Nevada County Library, and the Nevada County Friends of the Library, features local and regional musicians performing music geared toward youth yet enjoyable for all ages.

title: Paint What You Want: A 4-Week Guided Painting Workshop
description: Oil & Acrylic Adventures: Your Subject, Your Style. In-depth personalized painting instruction on the subject matter of your choice.

title: Intro to Stained Glass – Level 1
description: Experience the art of stained glass in this beginner-friendly workshop! Join us to craft a small suncatcher using vibrant glass pieces in this class with Charne Morris. No experience necessary—just bring your enthusiasm!, This introductory class focuses on straight cuts and will teach you the basics of stained glass safety.

title: Listening Landscapes Art Show
description: Listening Landscapes invites viewers into the quiet exchange between place, presence, and perception. The paintings are inspired by the simple act of sitting still within a landscape-listening before looking feeling before defining. Through layered color, movement, and abstraction, I translate the subtle sounds, rhythms, and emotions that rise from the land: wind, light, memory, silence. Each piece becomes a record of attention, a moment when the outer world meets the inner one.

title: THURSDAY NIGHT MARKET SUMMER SERIES ─ Grass Valley’s Favorite Summer Tradition is Back! Warm evenings, historic charm, and the buzz of community life—welcome to the Thursday Night Market Summer Series in Downtown Grass Valley!
description: Warm evenings, historic charm, and the buzz of community life—welcome to the Thursday Night Market Summer Series in Downtown Grass Valley! Every Thursday evening, our charming streets come alive with an eclectic mix of artisan vendors, farm-fresh produce, sizzling street food, and toe-tapping live music., Wander through local maker booths, sip something cool, and catch up with friends old and new. From handmade crafts and gourmet treats to the farm fresh produce at the Certified Farmers' Market and family fun, there’s something for everyone. Whether you’re a long-time local or a first-time visitor, this is summer in Grass Valley at its best—vibrant, friendly, and unforgettable.

title: Getting to Net Zero with Washoe County Sustainability Manager Brian Beffort
description: Washoe County’s Climate Action Plan aims to reduce greenhouse gas emissions to net zero by 2050. Brian Beffort, Washoe County Sustainability Manager, will share how it addresses the cost of living, rising heat, wildfire risk and air quality, and actions you can take to build a climate-resilient household and community. , This is part of an Art Bite series featuring local conservation and sustainability organizations, which complements Into the Time Horizon.

title: Summer Nature Pop Art Classes
description: Nature Pop Art classes, all ages, $75 for 3 sessions (2 hours each).

title: Playing and Creating Original Rhythms -A Monthly Playshop
description: We all have tapped out hands on our legs or on a table. You HAVE rhythm. Now, how about making a song out of it, adding layers of beats. I'll show you how! NO previous music experience required. Handout given at class with email follow-up.

title: Cars and coffee grass valley
description: Cars and coffee grass valley is a weekly car event at daily donuts in the Mcknight crossing shopping center every saturday morning from 7 to 10

title: STEAM Exploration Saturdays
Introduction to indigo dyeing
venue_name: Siren Song Fleece Works at Lucchesi Vineyards
description: Come and join us for an introduction into the magic of indigo, a plant dye used throughout the tropical and semi-tropical regions of the world for centuries. We will use some basic tie dye wraps to prepare a tshirt to dip into the best natural blue dye available. Materials provided.

title: Paint a collaborative wall mural at Lucchesi Vineyards
description: A hands-on wall mural workshop will be offered at Lucchesi Vineyards in Grass Valley California. Gary Graham, a well-known muralist, will instruct the various procedures required to create a unique work of art. This workshop will allow you to participate in all stages of creating a wall mural, from design and drawing to the painting itself., Each day of this six-day workshop will focus on a different element of the wall mural creation. You can attend one day, a weekend, or all of the sessions. Each day is a separate ticketed event, so you can pick and choose for your schedule and interests., The workshop dates and topics are as follows:July 11 - Design process + drawing (including color, perspective, scale), July 12 - Design process + drawing (including color, perspective, scale), July 18 - Paint (underpainting), July 19 - Paint (underpainting), July 25 - Paint (overpainting), July 26 - Paint (overpainting + glazing)

title: Hands ON! Second Saturday
description: Estelle J. Kelsey Foundation Hands ON! Second Saturdays offers monthly free admission, hands-on art activities, storytelling, a docent-guided tour, live performances, and community collaborations. “Art Expeditions” provides children the opportunity to engage with Museum staff and take a closer look at current work on view. New exhibitions, community collaborations, guest performances and monthly themes rotate in this engaging series of monthly programs. This month’s theme “Fruitful Summer” is inspired by Fallen Fruit’s Monument to Sharing.

title: In the Night Dimension and Those We Meet at Twilight

title: MIG Welding for Beginners and Artists Workshop
description: Get ready to put the Pedal to the Metal!, If you are an artist wanting to explore 3D metal art, need ranch repairs, or are a tinkerer wishing to add the strength and rigidity that metal offers, this is the workshop for you. Learn Welding Basics in this MIG Welding Class. No experience is necessary., New Expanded Content. This is a two-session class. 8 hours total instruction (must attend both days) , You will walk away with confidence in using MIG welding equipment, understanding safety protocols, setting up your workspace, and completing projects at home., This workshop is MIG Welding Qualification for Members. For member qualification, please arrange to complete a practicum with the facilitator and take the quiz after completing this course. , Hands-on learning:Safety equipment, safe practices, equipment selection, Equipment setup and joint prep, Material identification and preparation, Troubleshooting common problems, Distortion mitigation strategies, All equipment provided., Ages 14+ and Adults , Minimum participants required: 4 | Maximum: 6 (Please note: If the minimum participant count of 4 is not met, this class will be postponed to a later date.), Price: $255 & $30 materials

title: Flameworking Hot Links: The Art of Glass Chains
description: Hot Links: The Art of Glass Chains

title: Rockin' the Gateway presents "Life In The Fast Lane"
description: Life In The Fast Lane, the best Eagles tribute band in the country, returns to Western Gateway Park July 11! For over thirty years Life In The Fast Lane has been playing a 2+ hour note-for-note tribute to the Eagles and the solo work of Don Henley, Glenn Frey, and Joe Walsh. You will hear all of your favorite hits from “Take It Easy” to “Hotel California” with some surprises in between. Life In The Fast Lane does not simply play the music of the Eagles, they become the Eagles. Remember, at all Rockin’ the Gateway concerts children under 18 are free and leashed dogs are welcome! BYOB, sprits included, bring a picnic or visit one of our 3 food vendors. You’ll need a blanket or chair to sit on and of course your dancing shoes. The show starts at 7:30, after the sun is below the horizon, so all seating is in the shade. Tickets are only $25 at RockinTheGateway.com or $30 (cash only) at the door. Be prepared to Take It To The Limit on July 11 with Life In The Fast Lane at Rockin’ the Gateway. Get your tickets today!

title: The Creative Labyrinth 101: Nature and Creativity Journal Making
description: The Creative Labyrinth: Nature & Creativity Journal Making, Create a beautiful hard-cover journal or sketchbook from start to finish in this hands-on book arts workshop., Using the versatile Australian Hinge Binding, you’ll learn how to construct a professional-quality journal with a hard cover and multiple removable signatures. This unique binding style allows pages to be added, rearranged, or replaced over time, making it ideal for journaling, sketching, note-taking, nature observations, or creative projects., No previous bookbinding experience is needed. All materials and supplies are provided, and you’ll leave with a finished handcrafted book ready to use., What You’ll LearnThe anatomy of a hard-cover book, How to create and assemble book covers, Folding and preparing signatures, Australian Hinge Binding techniques, Methods for adding, removing, and rearranging pages, What You’ll CreateOne hard-cover journal, Ten removable signatures (approximately 40 pages), A versatile book that can be used as a journal, sketchbook, creative notebook, or nature journal, Optional Follow-Up Workshop, Participants may continue their journey in a second workshop, where the finished books become vessels for creative exploration through prompts, imagery, storytelling, and reflective practices inspired by nature and creativity., Class Details, Date: July 12, Time: 12:00 p.m. – 3:00 p.m., Ages: Adults & teens 13+, Skill Level: Beginner-friendly, Fee: $75, Class Size: Maximum 8 participants, Optional Follow-Up Session: July 19, 12:00 p.m. – 3:00 p.m. ($75)

title: Ceramics 101 – Mud Therapy
description: Join us at The Curious Forge for Sunday Ceramics with Bob Davis!

title: Beginning Macrame
description: Beginning Macramé: Create Your Own Wall Hanging

title: Ceramic- Garden Art
description: Come in and make some Ceramic Garden Art with Bob Davis

title: Summer Nature Pop Art Classes
description: Nature Pop Art classes, all ages, $75 for 3 sessions (2 hours each).

title: Word Jam
description: Join the Word Jam that takes place at 7pm on the second and fourth Sundays. Participants get 5 minutes to read a piece they have written

title: The Tale of The Princess Kaguya (Kaguyahime no monogatari)
venue_name: The Onyx Downtown at the Nevada Theatre
description: 7/12 - Found inside a shining stalk of bamboo by an old bamboo cutter and his wife, a tiny girl grows rapidly into an exceptional young lady. The mysterious young princess enthralls all who encounter her – but ultimately, she must confront her fate, the punishment for her crime. Featuring the voice talents of James Caan, John Cho, Darren Criss, Lucy Liu, James Marsden, and Chloë Grace Moretz, director Isao Takahata’s final film was nominated for an Academy Award® and hailed as a masterpiece by critics.

title: Build Your Own Skateboard – Youth Maker XD Camp Experience
description: Build your dream skateboard at our summer camp! Learn woodworking skills and make a custom board that stands out with your unique laser cut designs., Using a vacuum press and multiple layers of veneer, create a custom board with a kick . You will learn wood equipment safety and use of band saws, panel saw, router, sanders as well as learn and use laser cutters. Finish up by laser etching your design onto your deck then stain, seal and assemble trucks, wheels and bearings. Your end product will be a high-end custom skateboard!, Our camps are designed to be both fun and educational, with experienced instructors who are passionate about sharing their skills and knowledge with young people. We take great care to create a welcoming and inclusive environment where every child can feel valued and supported., Advanced Wood Fabrication processes:Vacuum Forming, Hardwood Veneers, Router, Bandsaw, Drilling, CNC Laser Cutting, Ages 12+, 4-Day Camp | 3 hours a day, Monday – Thursday 9:00 AM to 12:00 PM each day, $295, Class 4 minimum/6 maximum participants. (please note: if attendance does not meet minimum, class will be rescheduled)

title: Figurine & Character Sculpting Youth Maker XD Camp Experience
description: Join us for an adventure in sculpting, shaping, and imagining! Polymer clay is the perfect, no-mess medium to explore one’s creativity., In this fun and flexible workshop, young adults will experiment with different texturing tools, colors, and techniques to bring their ideas to life – from silly creatures and tiny treasures to action figurines and small sculptures – whatever one imagines, we will bring to fruition. This workshop is geared towards teens – with more focused guidance and instruction regarding design, details, and finishing. The students will be encouraged to exercise their creativity in this un-rushed, engaging art workshop that unfolds over 4 days. Final pieces can be painted with a variety of available mediums. We will bake our creations during the workshop so everyone goes home with a finished, cured piece.

Masters of their craft travel from all over to share their culture and heritage with our beautiful community, along with our own fabulous local teachers and percussionists, to offer a truly inspiring and engaging experience

title: Intro to Screen Printing
description: Intro to Screen Printing, Step into the studio for a beginner-friendly introduction to the bold, hands-on world of screen printing. In this guided workshop, you’ll discover how ink, a screen, and a squeegee can come together to create clean, professional prints — and you’ll walk away with a custom Curious Forge t-shirt that’s entirely your own., Through demonstration and hands-on practice, you’ll learn the fundamentals of the screen printing process from start to finish. We’ll cover fabric and ink types, proper printing form, squeegee technique, equipment maintenance, and ink curing — everything you need to understand the craft and feel confident doing it again on your own., No experience necessary. This class is designed for curious beginners, creative minds ready to try something new, and anyone who’s ever wanted to know how their favorite printed tee was made. It’s also family friendly — teens and adults are welcome., Details:Total Class Duration: 2 Hours, Who: Beginners, families, and the print-curious, Price: $40 class fee + $20 materials = $60 total, Ages: Adults and Teens 12+, Class Size: Minimum 4 / Maximum 8 participants, What to Wear: Please wear clothing you don’t mind getting ink on — screen printing is a hands-on process and stray ink happens to the best of us., Please note: All classes require a minimum number of participants and may be rescheduled if that minimum is not met. If this happens, we’ll let you know in advance and work with you to get you into the next available session.

title: "Musical Mornings" on Whimsical Wednesdays - Buffalo Gals
description: Free to the public, Musical Mornings is a popular, family-friendly summer series of one-hour morning concerts for listeners of all ages. The atmosphere is casual, with attendees bringing their own lawn chairs or blankets and snacks. Kids are encouraged to wiggle, dance, sing, and play., The summer series, presented by InConcert Sierra, the Nevada County Library, and the Nevada County Friends of the Library, features local and regional musicians performing music geared toward youth yet enjoyable for all ages., The Buffalo Gals are a beloved Americana and old-time string band based in Nevada City, California. Known for their traditional bluegrass twang, country rags, and vibrant harmonies, the group frequently performs at local venues, farmers' markets, and community fundraisers. Margo Meredith: fiddle & vocals; Jonathan Meredith: guitar; Ann Meigs: mandolin; Michael "Banjolio" Bremer: banjo & resonator guitar; Philip Wright: Bass

title: In the Night Dimension and Those We Meet at Twilight
description: Strange beings lurk in pixelated places, eyes peep from dark spaces, and seemingly random objects confront curious travelers. These are the dreamscapes that visitors will encounter in artist Jon Hioki’s new exhibition, In the Night Dimension and Those You Meet at Twilight, opening June 20th at Wonder Docent. The show, which features original paintings and sculptures, will be on display at the downtown Nevada City art supply store through August 16th., Wonder Docent will host an opening reception for In the Night Dimension and Those You Meet at Twilight on Saturday, June 20th from 6 to 8 pm.

title: Lavender Glow | Queer Art & Our Shared History
description: Beginning with Nisenan history into the present day, Lavender Glow reflects upon and celebrates the lived experiences and legacies of queer individuals and groups whose collective influence demonstrate a profound investment in place, arts, and culture. Alongside highlights of local history, Lavender Glow features 44 artworks selected from 127 submissions.

title: Figurine & Character Sculpting Youth Maker XD Camp Experience
description: Join us for an adventure in sculpting, shaping, and imagining! Polymer clay is the perfect, no-mess medium to explore one’s creativity., In this fun and flexible workshop, young adults will experiment with different texturing tools, colors, and techniques to bring their ideas to life – from silly creatures and tiny treasures to action figurines and small sculptures – whatever one imagines, we will bring to fruition. This workshop is geared towards teens – with more focused guidance and instruction regarding design, details, and finishing. The students will be encouraged to exercise their creativity in this un-rushed, engaging art workshop that unfolds over 4 days. Final pieces can be painted with a variety of available mediums. We will bake our creations during the workshop so everyone goes home with a finished, cured piece.

title: Summer Nature Pop Art Classes
description: Nature Pop Art classes, all ages, $75 for 3 sessions (2 hours each).

title: Playwriting Group
description: Sent upon request. (We meet in each other's homes.) All playwrights welcome! Whether playwriting is new to you or you're already an accomplished playwright, come join this on-going bi-weekly writers' group. We'll spend our time focusing on our writing, but with that magical element of doing so within the presence of others. Come discover the amazing experience of writing within the context of a group. Bring your computer, notebook, iPad, or whatever you like to use for writing. We share resources, discuss our goals and challenges, even provide feedback, but the primary focus of the group is to work on your own play! We'll do a check-in/discussion at the beginning of the meeting, then write in silence, and check-out at the end of our session. Contact Kimberly for the location and to answer any questions you may have.

title: Karaoke - Grass Valley Brewing Co.
description: It’s Karaoke Night at GVBC with DJ Kenn!

title: Macrame a Plant Hanger
description: Come relax and create your own beautiful plant hanger! You will learn some of the history behind this historical art while learning basic knots and techniques using soft 100% cotton cords. Macrame is an ancient and calming hands-on art, consisting of a beautiful variety of half knot and square knot patterns. It’s as easy as tying your shoe!

title: The Steel Wheels
description: The Steel Wheels take the stage July 17 for an evening of roots-driven music that moves both heart and feet., Blending bluegrass, folk, and Americana, their sound is rich with storytelling, tight harmonies, and a rhythm that builds into something truly electric live. It’s the kind of show that starts as a listening experience and ends with the whole room lifted., Reserved seating is filling steadily., Friday, July 17, Doors 7:00 PM | Show 8:00 PM, Secure your seats:, https://thecenterforthearts.my.salesforce-sites.com/ticket/#/events/a0SUZ000005JFBl2AO

title: Cars and coffee grass valley
description: Cars and coffee grass valley is a weekly car event at daily donuts in the Mcknight crossing shopping center every saturday morning from 7 to 10

title: STEAM Exploration Saturdays
Paint a collaborative wall mural at Lucchesi Vineyards
description: A hands-on wall mural workshop will be offered at Lucchesi Vineyards in Grass Valley California. Gary Graham, a well-known muralist, will instruct the various procedures required to create a unique work of art. This workshop will allow you to participate in all stages of creating a wall mural, from design and drawing to the painting itself., Each day of this six-day workshop will focus on a different element of the wall mural creation. You can attend one day, a weekend, or all of the sessions. Each day is a separate ticketed event, so you can pick and choose for your schedule and interests., The workshop dates and topics are as follows:July 11 - Design process + drawing (including color, perspective, scale), July 12 - Design process + drawing (including color, perspective, scale), July 18 - Paint (underpainting), July 19 - Paint (underpainting), July 25 - Paint (overpainting), July 26 - Paint (overpainting + glazing)

title: Family Fun Day | Summer Cooldown Edition 2026
description: Offered two times a year, these are free all-day events geared toward families and introduces many young children in our community to their first-ever arts experience. We offer families the opportunity to participate in healthy art-based activities together, such as face painting, crafts, and a balloon artist and other fun entertainment., , Events & Performers, Performances by Toni Tone, Face Painting by Lucy Galbraith & Sessapaints, Balloons by Bernard Bradshaw & Timothy Roberts, Arts & Crafts with Nancy Schaefer

title: In the Night Dimension and Those We Meet at Twilight
description: Strange beings lurk in pixelated places, eyes peep from dark spaces, and seemingly random objects confront curious travelers. These are the dreamscapes that visitors will encounter in artist Jon Hioki’s new exhibition, In the Night Dimension and Those You Meet at Twilight, opening June 20th at Wonder Docent. The show, which features original paintings and sculptures, will be on display at the downtown Nevada City art supply store through August 16th., Wonder Docent will host an opening reception for In the Night Dimension and Those You Meet at Twilight on Saturday, June 20th from 6 to 8 pm.

title: Watercolor Exploration with Chappy Valente
description: Immerse yourself in the expressive world of watercolor painting! Whether you’re brand new to watercolor or looking to expand your skills, this hands-on class with artist and educator Chappy Valente will help you gain confidence and technique. Learn to paint with fluidity and intention as you explore the tools, papers, and brushwork that make watercolor such a luminous and rewarding medium., Each session is a stand-alone class with fresh projects and techniques—join once or come monthly to continue growing your watercolor practice., What’s the Project:, Create your own watercolor studies exploring washes, color mixing, and texture effects. Each class introduces a new focus—such as landscapes, botanicals, or abstract forms—to inspire your creativity., What Will They Learn:Brush control and paint-to-water ratios, Wash techniques (wet-on-wet, wet-on-dry, gradient), Paper types, brush styles, and pigment selection, Layering, and color blending, Tips for composition and adding expressive detail, Materials Needed:, All materials are provided for in-class use. Students are welcome to bring their own favorite brushes or watercolor set if desired., Skill Level / Prerequisites:, All skill levels welcome—from first-time painters to those continuing their watercolor journey., Class Length:, One 3-hour session, (Optional continuing classes offered monthly), Fee: $50 + $20 materials, Minimum 3 / Maximum 5 participants

title: Sound Blessings With Robin
venue_name: Inner Path, 300 Commercial Street, Nevada City, CA
description: Join in a magical sound experience created from crystal singing bowls, voice and other instruments. Your personally requested blessing will be infused into the sound. Lay back, relax and float on the vibrations.

title: Dance Because Cabaret Fundraiser
description: Let's have a party and raise some money to support dance in Nevada City! Expect nothing, receive all your heart desires. Good times!, Join us for an evening of cabaret style entertainment - song, dance, poetry, and surprise! Then push the chairs aside and vogue with us and dance the night away!, Dance Because artists mix and mingle with you as we prepare to get to work on our second annual Nevada Theatre showcase coming in January 2027. Our goal is to have a party and raise $5000 to support production expenses for this upcoming show - costumes, rehearsal space, promotion. Our January, 2026 Nevada Theatre show featured 18 choreographers and 45 dancers and sold out three nights! We're building a movement here, folks, and it's just getting better. Help us fulfill our vision to celebrate our human-ness and our vital connections through the dance experience., Featured artists in the cabaret include: Shari Brookler, Kirstin Brown, Elizabeth Castañeda, Isadora Goldschneider, Azriel LaMarca, Leo Lauchere, Juliet Lin, Mary McDonnell, Delana Motter, Cassie Powell, Erin Reynolds, Sister Magic, and Trina Spiller, Tickets $35 for the whole evening.
